title: Apple strudel
subtitle: Filo pastry rolled around spiced apple and raisin filling
excerpt: Apple strudel made with filo pastry: eating apples sliced thin and mixed with raisins, cinnamon, and sugar, rolled inside four buttered sheets of filo, and baked until golden and flaky. The filo version is much simpler than the stretched strudel dough original, and the result is excellent.
sourceNotes: Apple strudel (Apfelstrudel) is an Austrian pastry documented from the seventeenth century, with roots in Ottoman baklava traditions. The canonical version uses a hand-stretched strudel dough; the simplified filo pastry version is a widely reproduced domestic adaptation. The filling of apple, raisins, sugar, and cinnamon appears in Austrian, Hungarian, and German domestic cookery references throughout the nineteenth and twentieth centuries. The filo version is documented in British domestic baking guides from the late twentieth century and is fully public-domain.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Best eaten within a few hours of baking. Reheat at 160°C fan for 10 minutes.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, 30 to 35 minutes until deep golden

glossaryTerms.definition: When rolling a strudel with filo, lay the sheets on a clean tea towel rather than directly on a surface — the cloth lets you lift and roll the strudel without the pastry cracking. Brush each sheet generously with melted butter before adding the next. Spread the filling across the lower third, leaving a 3 cm gap at each end to prevent leaking. Use the cloth to roll the strudel away from you, tucking in the ends as you go. Transfer seam-side-down onto the baking tray.

content.text: Filo strudel is one of the fastest pastry bakes. Twenty minutes of prep, 35 minutes in the oven. The key is slicing the apples thinly and squeezing out some of the moisture so the filling does not make the base filo soggy.

Toss the sliced {{apple-eating}} with the {{raisins}}, {{caster-sugar}}, {{cinnamon-ground}}, and the juice of the {{lemon}}. Leave to macerate for 10 minutes. Tip into a sieve and press to remove some of the released juice.
Preheat the oven to 190°C fan. See the

: lay a sheet of {{filo-pastry}} on a clean cloth and brush with melted {{unsalted-butter}}. Layer the remaining 3 sheets, buttering each. Spread the filling across the lower third of the strudel, leaving 3 cm at each end. Roll up using the cloth, tucking in the ends. Transfer seam-side-down to a lined baking tray. Brush the top with the remaining butter.
Bake for 30 to 35 minutes until the pastry is deep golden. Leave to cool for 10 minutes before dusting with {{icing-sugar}} and slicing. Serve warm with crème fraîche or pouring cream.
